story: I step into the dark house and find Luis Vasquez by the fire. He mocks my school, boasts of a long, strange life—sunlight not his friend. He threatens me with an iron poker and asks the purpose of life. I answer “to live,” and he spares me. Then he recalls the debt; my father sent me with payment. Outside in the snow, I open my trunk: Leo, my classmate, taped and panicked. I drag him to the porch; Luis hauls him inside and lays him on a granite table. Heavy curtains seal the windows; a pale smear of dawn edges them. Wolves howl. Leo pleads as Luis bends toward his throat.
question: As Luis leans in to take him, what will Leo most likely try to do to save himself?
answer_with_knowledge: Lunge for the curtains and try to tear them down to expose Luis to the coming sunlight.
answer_without_knowledge: Keep pleading and praying, begging Luis for mercy.
